close_reason: Arc<Client> is correct: Client is internally Arc-wrapped, double Arc is standard pattern
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:49 — 'client: Arc<aws_sdk_kms::Client>'. aws_sdk_kms::Client is internally a thin wrapper around an Arc<...Handle> (see aws-sdk-kms 1.105 Client docs: 'Client can be cheaply cloned and re-used'). Wrapping it in another Arc adds one atomic refcount layer with no shared-ownership benefit — every clone of the outer Arc could just be a clone of Client directly.

What goes wrong: nothing functional; pure noise. A serious Rust reader pauses to ask 'what shared mutable state is being protected here?' and finds none.

Why filed as opinion: the decision record at F13 (crate-secretsx-vfxr) defends the current shape for cross-crate uniformity. This finding is the opposing argument; the user can pick one. Do not file the opposite finding twice — close one as 'won't fix'.

If kept: leave a comment '// Arc<Client> is for uniformity with sister AWS backends; Client is internally Arc'd already.'
title: Arc<aws_sdk_kms::Client> is doubly reference-counted
